This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. 2026-03-24T18:51:50.9923934Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-24T18:51:02.599780602Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":2,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. 2026-03-24T18:51:51.0073093Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-24T18:51:35.050058846Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57412,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-24T18:51:51.0092323Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-24T18:51:35.345220385Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57412,\"title\":\"[BUG] StackOverflowException in Azure.Identity when CancellationToken is cancelled during in-flight GetTokenAsync on constrained stack environments\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Identity 1.12.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen `CancellationTokenSource.Cancel()` is called while `ClientCertificateCredential.GetTokenAsync()` is in-flight (e.g., waiting on a slow or unresponsive authority endpoint), the cancellation triggers synchronous continuation callbacks that produce an extremely deep call stack. On environments with constrained stack sizes (e.g., .NET Framework 4.8 with a 256KB–512KB stack), this causes a `StackOverflowException`.\\n\\nThe root cause is that `CancellationTokenSource.Cancel()` synchronously invokes all registered continuations on the current thread\\u0026#39;s stack frame. The Azure.Identity library\\u0026#39;s internal exception handling and retry logic during cancellation creates a very deep chain of synchronous continuations, exhausting the stack before control returns to the caller.\\n\\nThis is a problem for SDK consumers (e.g., Azure Cosmos DB .NET SDK) that pass a `CancellationToken` to `GetTokenAsync` and may need to cancel it during client disposal or shutdown. Even offloading `Cancel()` to a thread pool thread via `Task.Run()` does not help, because the thread pool thread\\u0026#39;s stack is also exhausted by the depth of the continuation chain.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nCalling `CancellationTokenSource.Cancel()` while `GetTokenAsync` is in-flight should gracefully cancel the operation and throw `OperationCanceledException` without exhausting the stack, regardless of the thread\\u0026#39;s stack size.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nA `StackOverflowException` is thrown during `CancellationTokenSource.Cancel()`. The stack trace shows deeply nested exception handling within Azure.Identity\\u0026#39;s internal credential pipeline (MSAL HTTP client, retry handlers, etc.).\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nA full reproduction project is available at: https://github.com/trivediyash_microsoft/TrafficManager-PoC/tree/cosmosdb-stackoverflow\\n\\nThe repository contains:\\n- **`StackOverflowRepro/`** — A .NET Framework 4.8 console app that creates a `ClientCertificateCredential` pointing to a slow/unresponsive authority, starts `GetTokenAsync`, and cancels the `CancellationToken` after 100ms. The binary has its stack size reduced to 256KB via `editbin /STACK:262144` to simulate constrained environments such as IIS.\\n- **`MockServer/`** — An ASP.NET Core HTTPS server that mocks `login.microsoftonline.us` with a ~20 second delay, triggering the in-flight cancellation scenario.\\n\\n### Setup\\n\\n1. Run the mock server certificate setup: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\MockServer\\\\setup-certificate-user.ps1`\\n2. Add `127.0.0.1 login.microsoftonline.us` to `C:\\\\Windows\\\\System32\\\\drivers\\\\etc\\\\hosts`\\n3. Update the certificate thumbprint in [`StackOverflowRepro\\\\Program.cs L:26`](https://github.com/trivediyash_microsoft/TrafficManager-PoC/blob/[REDACTED]/StackOverflowRepro/Program.cs#L26) to match the created certificate\\n4. Start the mock server: `cd MockServer \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; dotnet run`\\n5. Build and run `StackOverflowRepro` \\n   1. **Debug|x64 in Visual Studio**\\n   2. **rom developer Powershell for VS**\\n      ```\\n      msbuild .\\\\StackOverflowRepro.sln /p:Configuration=Debug /p:Platform=x64\\n      .\\\\StackOverflowRepro\\\\bin\\\\x64\\\\Debug\\\\StackOverflowRepro.exe\\n      ```\\n6. Observe `StackOverflowException` at `cts.Cancel()`\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n- **OS:** Windows 10/11\\n- **Runtime:** .NET Framework 4.8 (netstandard2.0 target)\\n- **Stack size:** 256KB (reduced via `editbin /STACK:262144`) — simulates IIS server environments\\n- **Azure.Identity:** 1.12.0\\n- **Azure.Core:** 1.44.1\\n- **Microsoft.Identity.Client (MSAL):** 4.63.0\\n- **IDE:** Visual Studio 2022/2026\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57412\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"yash2710\",\"id\":8458233,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/yash2710\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8458233?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"labels\":[\"Azure.Identity\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-24T18:49:16Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-24T18:49:16Z\",\"issue_type\":\"Bug\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
